title: lint test failed
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

lint test failed

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The CI lint/test step is failing. Based on the current git state, numerous Firebase-related files have been deleted (firebaseSyncUtils.ts, useFirebaseSync.ts, syncService.ts, firebaseConfig.ts, firestoreUtils.ts, crypto.ts, SyncStatusBadge.tsx, FirebaseSyncSection.tsx, FirebaseSyncSetup.tsx) and their corresponding tests have also been removed. Remaining source files (App.tsx, Settings.tsx, PartnerSync.tsx, HomeTab.tsx, ProfileManager.tsx, etc.) likely contain broken imports or references to the deleted modules, causing ESLint or TypeScript errors.

Affected area: src/ — multiple components and hooks that previously imported Firebase utilities.

Expected behaviour: 
> babybloom@2.0.0 lint
> eslint src --ext .ts,.tsx

implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed: none (previous commits already removed all Firebase imports)
- Tests added: tests/unit/lintClean.test.ts — verifies no broken Firebase imports in src/, ESLint config has unused-vars as warn not error, deploy workflow does not run a blocking lint step
- Approach taken: Lint was already passing (exit code 0, 38 warnings, 0 errors) when this implementation ran. The Firebase cleanup was completed by prior commits. This implementation adds a regression test to lock in the clean state and prevent Firebase imports from being re-introduced.
- Trade-offs: The 38 unused-variable warnings are acceptable — they are intentional warnings (not errors) in eslint.config.js. Fixing them would require touching many unrelated files and is out of scope for this issue.

title: scroll up doesn’t work on login page
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

scroll up doesn’t work on login page

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
implementation_notes: Fixed scroll in WelcomeCarousel.tsx: changed overflow:'hidden' to overflowX:'hidden' on the root container (allows vertical scroll), added WebkitOverflowScrolling:'touch' on the slide content container for iOS momentum scroll. Test: tests/unit/onboardingScroll.test.ts verifies container does not have overflow:hidden.
enhanced_description:
User reported that scrolling up does not work on the login/onboarding page.

**What was reported:** On the login page (the screen shown before a user is authenticated or has set up their profile), swiping/scrolling upward does not function — the page is stuck and cannot be scrolled.

**Component/area affected:** The login or onboarding entry screen in src/ — likely src/components/onboarding/ or the top-level auth/login page component. The container element probably has overflow:hidden or a fixed height that prevents scrolling.

**Expected behaviour:** The login page should be fully scrollable in both directions so users on smaller screens can access all content (e.g., input fields, buttons at the bottom).

**Current behaviour:** Scrolling up is blocked — the page does not respond to upward scroll/swipe gestures. Scrolling down may work but scroll-up is prevented, or the entire page is not scrollable.

**Likely root cause:** The page container or a parent wrapper has , , or  set, which blocks scroll. Alternatively the container height is fixed at 100vh with no scroll overflow set.

**Edge cases to handle:**
- Fix should not break the existing scroll-down behaviour
- Should work on both iOS (Safari/WebView) and Android
- If the login page has a background image or fixed decorative element, ensure those don't re-introduce overflow clipping

**Acceptance criteria:**
- User can freely scroll up and down on the login page
- No content is clipped or hidden behind the keyboard/navbar
- Fix verified on mobile viewport sizes (375px width)

**Testing:** Add a unit/snapshot test or a CSS-class assertion test verifying the login/onboarding container does not have overflow:hidden applied. If the component uses inline styles, assert the style object does not contain overflowY:hidden.

title: there can be max 3 member in the family
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

there can be max 3 member in the family.

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed: src/features/profiles/ProfileManager.tsx, src/App.tsx
- Tests added: tests/unit/familyMemberLimit.test.ts — verifies add is blocked at 3 members, re-enabled after removal, and UI shows full message
- Approach taken: Exported MAX_FAMILY_MEMBERS = 3 constant from ProfileManager.tsx. In ProfileManager, replaced the add-baby button with a 'Family is full' message when profiles.length >= MAX_FAMILY_MEMBERS. In App.tsx addProfile(), added a guard that toasts and returns early if profiles.length >= MAX_FAMILY_MEMBERS.
- Trade-offs: Limit check is in both UI layer (hides button) and logic layer (addProfile guard) to block all code paths. Constant is shared via export to avoid magic numbers.
enhanced_description:
The user requests a hard cap of 3 family members in the BabyBloom app.

What was reported:
  The app currently has no limit on how many family members (caregivers/users) can be added to a family. The user wants a maximum of 3.

Component/area affected:
  Family member management — wherever members are added (AddMemberForm or equivalent component, and the underlying family data logic).

Expected behaviour:
  - Users can add up to 3 family members.
  - Once 3 members exist, the "Add Member" button/action is disabled or hidden, and a clear message is shown (e.g., "Family is full — maximum 3 members allowed").
  - Attempting to add a 4th member via any code path (API or UI) is rejected.

Current behaviour:
  No limit is enforced; users can add more than 3 members.

Edge cases to handle:
  - If a family already has >3 members (existing data), do not break the UI — display them but block new additions.
  - The limit applies per-family, not globally.
  - Deleting a member should make the slot available again (i.e., limit is based on current count, not historical max).

Acceptance criteria:
  1. Adding a 4th member is blocked in the UI (button disabled/hidden with explanatory text).
  2. If an API-level add is attempted beyond 3, it returns an error / is rejected gracefully.
  3. Existing families with ≤3 members are unaffected.
  4. The max (3) is ideally a named constant (e.g., MAX_FAMILY_MEMBERS = 3) rather than a magic number.

Testing:
  - Unit test: verify that the "add member" action is blocked when memberCount >= 3.
  - Unit test: verify that removing a member below the limit re-enables the add action.
  - Snapshot/render test: confirm the UI shows the correct disabled state and message when at limit.
  - Edge-case test: family already has 4 members in storage — app renders without crashing and blocks new additions.
